column4: Request / Question
column9: Seller's Response
column10: Buyer's Follow-Up Comments

column4: Can you confirm the process mechanics - bid date, number of rounds, diligence window, and the target sign/close - and whether a lead bidder can get exclusivity? Is there any flexibility beyond a 100% cash asset sale (e.g., seller note, phased takedown)?
column9: Two-stage auction; initial bids targeted ~mid-July; CIM/VDR provided post-NDA in round one; sellers' preference is a 100% cash asset sale.
column10: Need the bid deadline and diligence window in writing, and whether exclusivity opens up after round one.

column4: Please load the full title commitment with Schedule B exceptions and confirm the closing mechanics to deliver clean title - payoff/discharge of the Bank OZK loan (~$52.6M) and release of the Sunbelt Rentals mechanics lien (~$30.5K).
column9: WF Industrial XII LLC is fee owner with good & marketable title; existing indebtedness expected to be paid off at closing from sale proceeds.
column10: Confirm the OZK discharge and Sunbelt lien release will be in hand at closing.

column4: Can you share the IDA documents (Company Lease, Lease & Project Agreement, PILOT schedule) and walk us through the consent path for a change of use and change of control - including any recapture exposure if we convert to data center use?
column9: Property subject to a Town of Brookhaven IDA lease/leaseback (WF remains fee owner); IDA defines the project as warehouse/distribution; IDA consent required for change of use; a sale without consent can trigger recapture; seller in discussions with TOBIDA.
column10: Need the executed IDA instruments and TOBIDA's written position on consent/recapture.

column4: Please provide queue-position evidence for NYISO Q#1721 and the draft Interconnection Agreement when available, and confirm the IA and queue position transfer on a change of control without a restudy or timeline reset.
column9: IA application submitted 10/30/2024 under Q#1721; IA expected to execute Q3 2026 for 177 MW; Facilities Study Agreement is binding on successors and assigns; seller holds biweekly PSEG-LI meetings and is on schedule; cited as the only DC in the LI queue per NYISO's public queue.
column10: Want written confirmation a buyer inherits the position cleanly, plus the public NYISO queue cite.

column4: Help us reconcile the load basis - the CIM cites 177 MW but the SIS studies 176.6 MW. Which figure carries into the IA and ESA, and would any increase trigger a NYISO restudy?
column9: Final SIS supports a 176.6 MW studied load with no significant adverse reliability impacts; any load revision requires NYISO review and may require a limited restudy.
column10: Confirm the contracted MW figure that will sit in the IA/ESA.

column4: This is our biggest open item: please reconcile the energization timeline. LIPA's SIS estimates ~39 months from IA execution for the substation and system upgrades, but the CIM markets first power in 2027 and your note suggests a ~12-month buyer self-build. What's the realistic in-service date, and what specifically drives the 39 months - the system upgrades (SUF/CTOAF/SASUF) or the substation itself?
column9: LIPA gave a non-binding estimate of 39 months from IA completion for the substation and relevant upgrades; alternatively the buyer (or LIPA-approved 3rd party) can build the substation in ~12 months with LIPA-approved design; SIS says PSEG-LI makes power available upon substation completion; the 90 MW (2027)/86 MW (2028) ramp is indicative, not contractual.
column10: Need the upgrade scope and sequence behind the 39 months, and a realistic energization date we can underwrite.

column4: Clarify the substation ownership and build split: materials indicate PSEG-LI owns the switching station/substation while the buyer funds and builds it. Who actually constructs what, and how does that interact with the 12- vs 39-month paths?
column9: On-site 138 kV 2N design: ~3.5-ac transformer/switchgear yard + ~1-ac PSEG-LI switching substation, 4 x 66 MVA; PSEG-LI will own the substation, customer owns downstream equipment; procurement/construction is the buyer's responsibility; design can't finalize until the facility study completes (~Aug 2026).
column10: Need the ownership/build boundary in writing and how it gates the timeline.

column4: Which substation budget is the basis - Clune (~$69.4M) or Hunter Roberts (~$84.0M)? And please share long-lead procurement status, quotes, and lead times for the 4 x 66 MVA transformers, HV breakers, and switchgear.
column9: Seller cited a Clune ROM of ~$70M (~$50M electrical, ~$10M GC, ~$5M site & building, ~$5M contingency) and ~1-year construction.
column10: Hunter Roberts (~$84M) is also in the room - which governs? Transformer lead time is the critical path; has anything been ordered or quoted?

column4: Please provide the draft Electric Service Agreement and confirm the all-in rate, term, escalation, and any credit/security. The CIM cites ~$0.15/kWh under Rate 285 'subject to the executed ESA' - what collateral sizing and CIAC/transmission security are payable?
column9: A 177 MW load falls under Rate 285 (large commercial); the ~$0.15/kWh delivery rate is subject to confirmation under the executed ESA (expected Q4 2026).
column10: Need the ESA draft, collateral sizing, and the CIAC figure.

column4: Can you confirm the SIS's ~$25.34M (+/-50%) LIPA upgrade cost estimate and how it's allocated between LIPA and the customer, and share the Facilities Study cost/schedule output when it lands?
column9: Facilities Study Agreement required a $90K deposit; study runs ~150 days to draft and ~180 days to final; cost allocation not yet bound.
column10: Need the bound upgrade cost and allocation once the Facilities Study completes (~Aug 2026).

column4: Given S10642 passed both houses on 6/4-6/5/26, what's your read on signing vs. veto timing, and would you accept a regulatory closing condition / outside-date relief / price adjustment tied to the outcome?
column9: Seller's advisors expect Senate passage but anticipate a Hochul veto; she has until 12/31/26 and is viewed as unlikely to act before the November midterms; seller is open to entertaining a regulatory closing condition.
column10: We'll want this reflected in the PSA - confirm the seller will paper a regulatory CP.

column4: Please share your counsel's grandfathering analysis: does the completed warehouse construction satisfy 'commencing construction,' and which existing campus permits can be modified (rather than newly issued) under the DEC moratorium's exceptions?
column9: Site already holds local/county/utility approvals (SCWA Aug'22, Pine Barrens Nov'22, SCDHS Nov'22, Planning Board Dec'22, NYSDOT Dec'22, tree-clearing/site-work/building permits, temp CO Apr'24, final CO Jun'24); substation/fitout still need Town permits; a Minor Site Plan is under review.
column10: Need the written grandfathering opinion and the modify-vs-new permit list.

column4: How do you see the permanent S10642 obligations applying here - host-community-benefit funding via the LIPA program, prevailing wage/PLA and domestic steel for the retrofit, and the renewable-supply standard (>=1/3 by 2030 rising to 90% by 2040)? What's the compliance plan?
column9: Town to hold a public hearing July 16 on an 18-month data center moratorium; seller reports supportive local officials and a planned PR/legal/technical response; fuel-cell and geothermal generation concepts and a capture-and-use system for cooling water are being evaluated.
column10: Need a quantified view of the host-community/renewable/labor obligations and the generation architecture assumed.

column4: Confirm the remaining Town conversion path: what site-plan amendment scope (substation, generator yard, cooling plant), building permits, and SEQRA determination does the data-center use still require, and where does the Minor Site Plan application stand?
column9: Land-use counsel memo confirms data center qualifies as R&D, a permitted use in the L-1 district; a Minor Site Plan application is under review; Town hearing July 16.
column10: Need the remaining-approvals matrix with a timeline and any SEQRA trigger.

column4: Please identify the exact aquifer designation (Central Pine Barrens CGA / Special Groundwater Protection Area / Suffolk Art. 6) and provide the recorded Central Pine Barrens conservation easement and amendment, so we can overlay the protected/non-buildable area and any diesel/chemical-storage and stormwater-recharge limits onto the substation and generator yards.
column9: Seller notes a sewage-density limit (added floor area requires density credits) and is 'not aware' of diesel-storage or stormwater limits.
column10: Need the recorded easement instrument and the formal designation; the 'not aware' answer needs to be backed by the actual restrictions.

column4: Can you refresh the environmental record for the as-built campus - an updated Phase I ESA (the 2022 report predates construction) and the wetlands delineation and T&E species report that are referenced but not in the room?
column9: Seller points to VDR folders 3/4/5; reports no RECs, open violations, or unresolved conditions; site was wooded forest before the warehouse development.
column10: 2022 Phase I is stale and pre-construction; wetlands delineation and species clearance aren't actually in the VDR.

column4: Beyond the substation, what's the remaining conversion capex to a powered shell, and has the geotech (designed for warehouse loads) been validated for data-center equipment - transformer/UPS/generator pads and liquid-cooling slab loads against the 7-inch / 800 PSF slab and 2 tsf bearing?
column9: Basis of Design (Syska-Gensler) in 2.1: 7-inch steel-fiber slab (800 PSF, FF45/FL35), 36' clear, 300 W/SF air + 600 W/SF direct-to-chip closed-loop, PUE 1.5, 117 IT MW total (A 44 / B 38 / C 35).
column10: Need a remaining-capex estimate and a geotech check for DC equipment loads vs. the warehouse-designed report.

column4: Confirm the backup-generation architecture assumed in the Basis of Design (diesel vs. fuel cell vs. BESS) and the resulting NYSDEC air-permit applicability in this severe-ozone-nonattainment area - that choice drives both moratorium exposure and the air-permit path.
column9: Title V air permit would be required if the chosen emergency generators trigger it; low-emission designs can avoid it; seller has a fuel-cell concept that should avoid NYSDEC air permits; a NYSDEC stormwater permit applies if >1 acre is disturbed.
column10: Need the emissions/applicability analysis and the architecture actually carried in the BoD.

column4: Please provide the SCWA will-serve letter and confirmed capacity on the 14-inch main, and - if a water-cooled configuration is ever contemplated - the cost/timeline for the sewer extension or on-site WWTP under the aquifer constraints.
column9: Base design is closed-loop with no external water supply or discharge; SCWA provides general water; a water-cooled option would need three 8-inch taps off the 14-inch mains and possibly a private WWTP/other upgrades.
column10: Need the SCWA will-serve letter and capacity confirmation.

column4: Can you share the carrier will-serve letters (AT&T, Cogent, Extenet, Lightpath, OCG, Verizon, Zayo), physically diverse route maps, and latency to the key NYC-metro interconnect points (60 Hudson, 111 8th Ave, NJ campuses)?
column9: Seven carriers within ~0.5 mi; carrier will-serve represented as provided; fiber appendix referenced in the Feasibility Study.
column10: Need the actual will-serve letters and route-diversity maps, not just the representation.

column4: Confirm there's no relationship, shared infrastructure, or queue competition with the separate ~228-acre 'Brookhaven Logistics Center' rezoning south of the LIE, and whether any expansion option or ROFO is available to a buyer.
column9: Seller states there is no relationship - it's a separate project and ownership entity on the south side of the Long Island Expressway; this site is on the north side.
column10: Confirmed separate; closed unless an expansion option becomes relevant.
